Title: Division 7A Loans - Debit Loan Warning
Content:
In the Financial Statements of the Company, we believe that the following amounts would be viewed by the ATO as “debit loan accounts”. This represents cash that has been drawn out of the Company by individuals (or associated Trusts) without personal income tax being paid. GIVE AN EXPLANATION - eg. The main contributor to this situation was the use of business funds to purchase your investment property.

DETAILSAMOUNT
LOANS - name$0
TOTAL$0

The effect of Division 7A on the above loan accounts is to deem these amounts as unfranked dividends (i.e. dividends with no imputation credits) paid from the Company to shareholders. This could result in a bad tax outcome, with shareholders possibly paying tax at the top marginal rate of 47%.

Title: Division 7A - Option 1: Payment of Franked Dividend
Content:
OPTION 1 - Payment of Franked Dividend

You could issue a franked dividend from your Company to remove this loan account. For example, as at 30 June 2024 this Company had $amount in retained profits (please refer to the attached Financial Statements). This means that a franked dividend could be issued as follows:

Dividend - Person 1$amount
Dividend - Person 2$amount
Total$amount

Your debit loan accounts would then reduce to $NIL. The estimated extra income tax to pay in your 2024 Income Tax Return would be approximately $Extra Tax.

The above tax calculation assumes gross wages of approximately $Wages Amount from Business Name.

Title: Division 7A - Option 2: Formalise Loan Agreement
Content:
OPTION 2 - Formalise the loans through a Loan Agreement

The above loans could be formalised through signing a Loan Agreement to repay interest and principal amounts back to the business. As they would be considered to be unsecured, they would need to be paid back over a 7-year period. The interest rate for the 2024 financial year is 8.27%. The minimum repayments are calculated under the relevant Tax Act provisions. This interest would not be tax deductible as it is not linked to any direct income producing investment.

Option 1 — Payment of Franked Dividend

You could issue a franked dividend from your Company to remove this loan account. For example, as at 30 June 2024 this Company had $amount in retained profits (please refer to the attached Financial Statements). This means that a franked dividend could be issued as follows:

Option 2 — Formalise the Loans through a Loan Agreement

The above loans could be formalised through signing a Loan Agreement to repay interest and principal amounts back to the business. As they would be considered to be unsecured, they would need to be paid back over a 7-year period. The interest rate for the 2024 financial year is 8.27%. How to Avoid This Situation in the Future

We recommend that you declare a wage from Business Name, which matches your consumption needs. Based on 2023 tax rates, if you need to live on say $60,853 (net after tax) then the wage to be declared in the trust is $80,000 (gross) and PAYG withholding of $19,147 needs to be paid. Also, superannuation of $8,800 (11% of your gross wages amount of $80,000) will have to be paid by the required payment deadline.

Title: Division 7A - Existing Loan Agreement
Content:
Division 7A — Existing Loan Agreement

Division 7A contains anti-avoidance provisions designed to prevent tax-free payments from private companies to associated persons i.e. “debit loans”. The effect of Division 7A is to deem these amounts as unfranked dividends (i.e. dividends with no imputation credits). The shareholder or their associated would need to include the full amount of the loan in their tax return.

This Company has entered into a Loan Agreement with the Trust Name for the repayment of their “debit loan” account in 2024. We have adjusted your 2024 Financial Statements and Income Tax Returns to ensure that the appropriate interest and capital repayments have been made on your “debit loan”.

DATEDESCRIPTIONREPAYMENTINTERESTBALANCE
datedescription$0$0$0

The loan will not be taken to be a dividend because the total repayments are greater than the minimum year repayment required for 2024.

Title: Payment of Company Tax
Content:
Companies are subject to full self-assessment which means that assessments (based upon the taxable income disclosed in returns) are deemed to be issued and the tax owing is due and payable upon lodgement of the Income Tax Return.

It is your responsibility to ensure that the amount of $amount is paid to the Australian Taxation Office by Due Date.

An income tax assessment notice will not be issued to the Company. Please use the enclosed payment advice and make your payment to the Australian Taxation Office by Due Date.

Title: Personal Services Income
Content: The purpose of the personal services income (PSI) rules is to ensure that income derived from personal services is taxed in the hands of the individual who performs the work, regardless of whether the income is derived by an individual, partnership, company or trust. The PSI rules do not apply if the entity is treated as carrying on a personal services business (by passing certain tests). It appears that your Company is carrying on a personal services business as it passes the INSERT TEST. We have prepared your 2024 Tax Return on this basis.

Title: Potential Insolvent Trading
Content:
You will observe from the enclosed Financial Statements that this Company had a working capital deficiency at the end of the 2024 financial year.

There is an obligation under the Corporations Law for the Directors to make a statement as to whether or not “there are reasonable grounds to believe that the company will be able to pay its debts as and when they fall due”.

If Directors knowingly make a false statement, they are in breach of the Corporations Law and may become personally liable for company debts. Likewise, if they make a statement that the company is unable to pay its debts but then continue to trade in that situation, they are also in breach of the Corporations Law.

If you consider, in view of the abovementioned deficiency, that your company is in this situation, it would appear that additional funds would need to be injected by the Directors/Shareholders in the form of subordinated loans or share capital. If by way of loans, the providers of such funds would then need to give an undertaking that they would not call up the loans unless all other creditors had been paid. The alternative is liquidation.

We strongly recommend that you consider our advice above, and then immediately contact our office if you would like to discuss this further.

Title: Accounting vs Taxation Income
Content:
You will notice that the profit on your Financial Statements of $amount differs from the profit on your Income Tax Return of $amount. This difference is made up of the following tax adjustments:

Unpaid Leave Entitlements:
Accrued leave entitlements refer to annual leave and long service leave payments that your employees are presently entitled to but which have not yet been taken. As the employer, you will be entitled to a deduction in respect of accrued leave entitlements when they are paid to the employee.

Unpaid Employee Superannuation:
Superannuation can only be claimed as a tax deduction when it is physically paid to the superannuation fund and clears your bank account.

Fines:
Fines are not tax deductible and therefore have been added back to your income for tax purposes.

Entertainment Expenses:
Certain entertainment expenses are not tax deductible and have therefore been added back for taxation purposes.

Franking Credits on Dividends:
The entity type has received franked dividends during the year. As only cash received for the dividend is included in accounting income, the franking credits attached to the dividend need to be added to assessable income. The franking credits then form part of the tax you have paid to ensure the dividend income is not taxed twice.

Instant Asset Write Off:
For the 2024 Financial Year, [Business Name] was eligible for the Instant Asset Write Off. This meant that your business was able to claim an immediate tax deduction for eligible assets and equipment purchased up to the threshold of $150,000 each during the year. Additionally, because your Small Business Pool Balance was less than $150,000 at the end of the financial year, we were able to write off the entire pool balance and save you tax.

Total Tax Saving = $x,xxx

Title: Cash Basis Adjustment
Content:
We have deducted the amounts owed by the Trade Debtors of your business as at 30 June 2024 from the revenue amount in the Income Tax Return of your business. This is because your Financial Statements are prepared on an accruals basis, whereas your taxable income is calculated on a cash basis. Under a cash basis, revenue is only recognised when cash is received from customers and expenses when they are paid to a supplier.

Therefore, while the net profit of your business was $amount, the taxable income of the business was $amount after adjusting for your trade debtors and creditors.

IMPORTANT NOTE: You will need to make adequate provision for increased tax payments in the future created by this adjustment.

TOTAL TAX SAVED - Timing Difference $amount

Title: Employee Superannuation - Shortfall
Content:
During the 2024 financial year, it appears that Business Name has not paid the required 9.5% employer superannuation contribution for employees. The shortfall of superannuation contributions for the 2024 financial year is $shortfall amount.

The above payments were due to be paid by 28 July 2024. Please note that if your superannuation payment was made AFTER 28 July 2024, but was made PRIOR to 28 August 2024, the payment can still be made to a superannuation fund, however a Superannuation Guarantee Charge Statement must be completed and sent to the ATO along with a Super Guarantee Late Payment Offset Election to allow your payment to offset any liability created by the Superannuation Guarantee Charge Statement.

Alternatively, should payment be made after 28 August 2024, the payment must be made direct to the ATO and remitted with a Superannuation Guarantee Charge Statement. If payment was also made to a superannuation fund, you will not be eligible for an offset and the full Superannuation Guarantee Charge will still be liable.

If you didn’t pay these amounts by 28 July 2024, please contact our office immediately to determine the best outcome for your situation, as these issues can be quite complex! If needed, we can also prepare the appropriate documents for you to send to the ATO along with your payment.

Title: Extended Time for Lodgement
Content:
Because Client Name has tax payable, lodgement can be delayed should you desire. When the payment is due, however, relies upon the actual lodgement date of the return:

  • If lodged before 12 February 2021, payment will be due by 21 March 2021
  • If lodged between 13 February and 12 March 2021, payment will be due by 21 April 2021
  • If lodged between 13 March and 15 May 2021, payment will be due by 5 June 2021

As you can see, there are plenty of options that allow you to dictate when you pay the ATO and can allow for you to have the funds working for you a little longer rather than for the government!

Would you please advise when you would like us to lodge Client Name’s return?

Title: Introducing Xero
Content:
We are now using Xero in our Accounting firm - Xero is an easy to use but powerful online accounting system that’s designed specifically for small businesses like yours.

Xero provides a view of financial information in real-time. There’s no need to buy expensive software and install upgrades. Xero is available on your PC or Mac in the office, at home or on popular mobile devices - anywhere there is an internet connection.

The privacy and security of your data is taken care of and is stored on Xero’s secure servers, where it’s backed up regularly. This means even if your computer is lost or stolen your data is safe.

Xero automatically imports your bank statements daily so you can keep abreast of your cashflow. Xero has a full suite of accounting features such as invoicing, payables, expense claims, GST, tax returns, reporting and much more.

The beauty of Xero is you can invite a number of trusted people such as us, your Accountants, to collaborate online. No more cumbersome transfer of data that can be corrupted or is out-of-date. We can both work on and view your accounting data at the same time!

We think your business is perfectly suited to using Xero, as a Xero Certified Partner, we can introduce you to Xero and have you up and running in no time. We will discuss this with you at our upcoming Annual General Meeting.

  • Ensure that all transactions entered into your Xero / MYOB / QuickBooks / XXXX system have accurate descriptions.

  • Ensure that all private expenses are removed from the business accounts and placed in the appropriate loan account.
  • Ensure that your Xero / MYOB / QuickBooks / XXXX file is up to date and contains all adjusting entries prepared by our office.
  • Ensure all balance sheet items at year-end are still in existence and are accurately recorded at cost.
  • For the next financial year, please ensure that the correct GST codes appear on each transaction.

Title: Issues & Recommendations - Header
Content: The issues listed below highlight problem areas and solutions to make things better. We will discuss these with you on our Annual Review Meeting.

Title: Additional Tax on Super for High Income Earners
Content:
Division 293 tax is an additional tax on super contributions which reduces the tax concession for individuals whose combined annual income and contributions are greater than $250,000.

If your annual income and super contributions exceeds $250,000, then you are taxed at 15% on either your contributions, or the amount that is over the threshold - whichever amount is lower.

We estimate you will need to pay

>$xxx on your superannuation contributions. The ATO will send you a Division 293 Tax Notice with their assessment of the amount payable after you lodge your annual Tax Return.

You can pay this Division 293 tax by:

  1. Payment from your personal bank account, or
  2. By electing to release money from any of your existing super balances by completing a Division 293 election form.
